title: Podman - IPv6 Address Selection favours ULA over GUA also for Remote Hosts
body:
As discussed with @sbrivio-rh over IRC, this is an Issue meant to track Pasta IPv6 Address Selection (no Time to fill the complete Issue Template right now).

Based on a quick Discussion this seems to be due to Passt / Pasta Commit a96a962d7e51 ("netlink: Don't require address to be global, just not link local") and ULA is preferred over GUA.

This is different to how Fedora GNU/Linux Host's (KVM/LXC) Address Selection works (GUA should be preferred **unless** IPv6 Target Address is also ULA and possibly in the same Subnet).

As a Consequence, Container can access only LAN-based Resources, but cannot access Public (Internet) Resources.

EDIT 1: it might be related to IPv6 non-local Bind (`net.ipv6.ip_nonlocal_bind=1`) since I do NOT experience this on an LXC container where Podman's Pod has Port-Forwarding based on a Host-bound IP Address.

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

This issue is caused by the address selection logic in pasta, which picks a single "best" IPv6 address from the host interface based on the longest prefix length.  Because of which ULA can get selected if it has longer prefix.

This is being addressed by Jon Maloy's patch series for introducing multiple address ( https://archives.passt.top/passt-dev/20260413005319.3295910-7-jmaloy@redhat.com/ ) , which is currently under review. With this changes, the container/guest will read all address instead of selecting the best one.

If you like to test whether this series resolves the issue on your setup, you can simply try it using:
```
git clone git://passt.top/passt && cd passt && b4 shazam https://archives.passt.top/passt-dev/20260413005319.3295910-1-jmaloy@redhat.com/ && make && sudo make install
``` 

podman will pickup this series changes automatically once you have done the `make install`. This can easily be reverted using `make uninstall` command.

It would be very helpful if you could confirm whether this fixes the issue for you.  Thanks:)
